text:
Market today: Mixed signals. Growth/tech names are getting a nudge from Tesla's FSD update (AI/autonomy optimism spills into semis and software), but the macro undertow is tricky - Supreme Court rulings expanding executive power over regulators adds uncertainty for tech-adjacent names. Not a rip-higher day for your book, more of a 'hold the line' session.

Your holdings: Three need eyes. AVGO (-14.66%) is your largest position and flagged TRIM - you're underwater and on margin, that's a double punch; stop at 336, know where you'd cut. PLTR (-11.08%) is flagged SELL - significant loss, no obvious catalyst today to reverse it; margin amplifies every dip here. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with thin data - Charter/SpaceX news is a minor positive but don't let a headline move keep you from managing size carefully.

Watch: 1) AVGO - any further fade toward 336 stop triggers a real decision. 2) Broader regulatory headlines - Supreme Court rulings on FTC/Fed could shift sentiment on high-multiple tech fast.

Bottom line: Your two biggest losers are on margin - decide your AVGO and PLTR exit levels now, not during a drop.
